message:
test(fcp-conformance/leaky-bucket): pin LeakyBucket flow-control under backpressure (br-ilmri)

LeakyBucket is the pacing-style rate limiter in fcp-ratelimit, sibling
of TokenBucket (already pinned in ratelimit_token_bucket_conformance).
Connectors rely on LeakyBucket for smoothing requests against an
upstream service. Zero conformance coverage today.

Adds 11 tests against the public RateLimiter contract:

- zero_permit_acquire_is_a_no_op
- exact_capacity_acquire_succeeds
- over_capacity_multi_permit_acquire_is_atomic (no partial
  consumption — silent drain defense)
- consecutive_acquires_drain_capacity_until_empty
- leak_recovers_capacity_after_elapsed_time (proportional refill)
- wait_time_is_zero_when_capacity_is_available
- wait_time_is_nonzero_when_bucket_is_full
- acquire_returns_wait_exceeded_when_max_wait_too_short (the
  deadline-honouring contract callers depend on)
- reset_clears_bucket_level_to_zero
- from_window_produces_equivalent_throughput_to_explicit_leak_rate
  (the two constructors must agree on steady-state rate)
- state_reports_limit_remaining_and_is_limited

All 11 pass locally in 0.16s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
ratelimit_leaky_bucket_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
